checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: No AccountsReceivable, LoansReceivable, or similar receivable line-item data was provided in the financial_series_point_in_time dataset for BXMT.

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: No IntangibleAssets, Goodwill, OtherAssets or capitalized cost concepts were provided in the dataset; only aggregate Assets is available.

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: BXMT is a commercial mortgage REIT; no Inventory or COGS/GrossProfit concepts are applicable or provided in the dataset.

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2013, quarterly NetIncomeLoss 2011 Q1 vs Q2

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=181024000 (2012 annual) vs NetIncomeLoss=15032000 (2013 annual) vs NetIncomeLoss=90045000 (2014 annual)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: No specific allowance-for-loan-losses or credit-reserve line item was provided in the dataset, limiting a reserve-specific assessment.

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filing_date 2015-02-17 for FY2014 (well within the standard 60-90 day deadline); no NT 10-K or NT 10-Q entries appear in the filing_chronology for 2011-2016.
10-K/A filings appear only in 1999-04-30, 2000-04-28, and 2004-04-29, all predating the review period (2011-2016); no 10-K/A or 10-Q/A occurs in 2011-2016.

Numerous IRANNOTICE and CORRESP/UPLOAD filings (routine SEC Iran-disclosure compliance and comment-letter correspondence) appear regularly from 2013-2015, consistent with normal review process rather than enforcement escalation.

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Dataset lacks receivables, intangible assets, goodwill, other-asset breakdowns, and allowance/reserve line items; also several concept series (e.g., Revenues) end at 2014-12-31 without full-year 2015 or CY2015 annual figures, and StockholdersEquity/Assets show extreme swings (e.g., Assets=4120690000 at 2010-12-31 vs Assets=322343000 at 2012-12-31 vs Assets=9851868000 at 2015-09-30) reflecting a major corporate recapitalization that complicates trend analysis.

top_signals: Large, multi-year divergence between NetIncomeLoss and NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities in 2011 (NetIncomeLoss=258142000 vs OperatingCF=28884000) and 2012 (NetIncomeLoss=181024000 vs OperatingCF=6768000), which narrows substantially by 2014 (NetIncomeLoss=90045000 vs OperatingCF=80637000), consistent with large non-cash/non-recurring gains during the legacy Capital Trust loan-portfolio wind-down and Blackstone recapitalization rather than core operating performance.

Extreme volatility in Assets, Liabilities and StockholdersEquity from 2010-2013 (e.g., Assets=4120690000 at 2010-12-31 collapsing to Assets=322343000 at 2012-12-31 then rebuilding to Assets=9851868000 by 2015-09-30) reflecting the 2013 Blackstone recapitalization of the former Capital Trust, which limits comparability of period-over-period trend-based checks (CL1, CL2, CL6).

Data gaps (no receivables, intangible/goodwill, or loan-loss-reserve concepts provided) prevent a full assessment of several standard earnings-quality checklist items, reducing overall confidence in either direction.
